title: Järjestysluvut
description: Learn Finnish ordinal numbers (1st, 2nd, 3rd...) and how to express dates
instructions: Practice forming ordinal numbers and using them in dates — an essential everyday skill!

questions.text: What is the basic rule for forming ordinal numbers in Finnish (4th and higher)?
options: Remove the last vowel and add -as
questions.explanation: The basic rule: add -s to the counting number. Example: neljä (four) → neljäs (fourth). Simple!

questions.text: What is 'neljäs' in English?
questions.explanation: 'Neljäs' = fourth. Formed from neljä (four) + s = neljäs.

questions.text: What is 'seitsemäs' in English?
questions.explanation: 'Seitsemäs' = seventh. Formed from seitsemän (seven) + s. But wait — seitsemän ends in -n, so drop the n: seitsemä + s = seitsemäs.

questions.text: Numbers ending in -n: what happens before adding -s?
questions.explanation: For numbers ending in -n (like kahdeksan = 8, yhdeksän = 9), drop the -n before adding -s. kahdeksan → kahdeksa + s = kahdeksas.

questions.text: Numbers ending in -si: what happens before adding -s?
questions.explanation: For numbers ending in -si (like viisi = 5, kuusi = 6), drop the -i before adding -s. viisi → viide + s = viides. The stem changes: viisi → viide-, kuusi → kuude-.

questions.text: What is 'kahdeksas' in English?
questions.explanation: 'Kahdeksas' = eighth. From kahdeksan (8): drop -n → kahdeksa + s = kahdeksas.

questions.text: What is 'viides' in English?
questions.explanation: 'Viides' = fifth. From viisi (5): drop -i, stem changes to viide- + s = viides.

questions.text: What is the ordinal form of 1 (first) in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Ensimmäinen' = first. This is completely irregular — does NOT follow the +s rule. Spoken Finnish: 'eka'.

questions.text: What is the spoken Finnish word for 'first'?
questions.explanation: 'Eka' = first (spoken Finnish). 'Ensimmäinen' is the formal/written form. Both are commonly used.

questions.text: What is 'toinen' in this context: 'Tämä on toinen kirja'?
options: Both A and B — 'toinen' means both!
questions.explanation: 'Toinen' means both 'second' AND 'other/another' depending on context. 'Toinen kirja' = the second book OR another book. Context tells you which meaning.

questions.text: What is 'kolmas' in English?
questions.explanation: 'Kolmas' = third. This is semi-irregular — it doesn't simply add -s to kolme. The -e changes to -as: kolm + as = kolmas.

questions.text: Match: 1st = ensimmäinen / eka, 2nd = toinen / toka, 3rd = ?
questions.explanation: 3rd = kolmas. The first three ordinals are all irregular in Finnish. After that (4th, 5th...) the +s rule applies regularly.

questions.text: What is the formula for dates in Finnish?
questions.explanation: Finnish dates: Ordinal number + month name + -ta. Since all Finnish months end in -kuu, the partitive is always -ta: tammikuuta, helmikuuta, etc.

questions.text: How do you say 'the 8th of June' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Kahdeksas kesäkuuta' = the 8th of June. Ordinal (kahdeksas) + month in partitive (kesäkuuta = kesäkuu + -ta).

questions.text: How do you say 'the 1st of January' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Ensimmäinen tammikuuta' = the 1st of January. The ordinal stays in nominative form as the date marker.

questions.text: Finnish writes dates with a period after the number. How is 15.5. read aloud?
questions.explanation: 'Viidestoista toukokuuta' = 15th of May. 15 = viidestoista (5th + ten = viides + toista). The period after 15.5. tells you it's a date, not a decimal.

questions.text: How do you say 'My birthday is the 1st of September'?
questions.explanation: 'Mun syntymäpäivä on eka syyskuuta.' = My birthday is the 1st of September. 'Eka' is the spoken form of 'ensimmäinen'. 'Syyskuuta' = syyskuu + -ta (partitive).

questions.text: How do you say 'the 2nd of February' in spoken Finnish? (Use toka)
questions.explanation: 'Toka helmikuuta' (spoken) or 'toinen helmikuuta' (formal) = the 2nd of February.

questions.text: What question word do you use to ask 'when?' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Milloin?' = When? Used for asking about dates and times. Example: 'Milloin sun syntymäpäivä on?' (When is your birthday?)

questions.text: What does 'Kuinka mones?' or 'Monesko?' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Monesko?' or 'Kuinka mones?' = What date? / What position? These are the question forms for ordinal numbers. Example: 'Monesko päivä tänään on?' (What's today's date?)

questions.text: How do you ask 'When is your birthday?' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Milloin sun syntymäpäivä on?' = When is your birthday? You can also say 'Koska sun syntymäpäivä on?' (slightly more informal).

questions.text: What is the ordinal form of 'kymmenen' (ten)? → _____
questions.explanation: kymmenen → drop -n → kymmen + es = kymmenes (tenth).

questions.text: What are the spoken Finnish words for 1st and 2nd?
questions.explanation: Spoken Finnish: 1st = eka, 2nd = toka. These are common in everyday speech. Formal: ensimmäinen and toinen.

questions.text: What is the partitive ending for Finnish month names (like tammikuu)?
questions.explanation: All Finnish months end in -kuu, so the partitive singular is always -ta: tammikuuta, helmikuuta, maaliskuuta... This makes dates easy once you know the months!
